document.title: M1 Lattice: Transport Specification
document.purpose: Defines the transport and object storage model for M1 Lattice data, including file-level semantics, composition rules, identity resolution, and loading behavior.

overview: The M1 transport model defines how M1 data is represented, stored, and exchanged between systems. Transport units are designed as composable fragments of a global identity-merged graph. Files do not represent closed datasets or authoritative sources, but instead contribute knowledge to a shared, distributed structure.
core_principles: M1 is a non-authoritative, identity-merged distributed graph.
Transport units contribute knowledge; they do not define ownership or completeness.
All data is merged into a single conceptual global graph via identity.
Identity is authoritative; all other data is subject to merge rules.
Systems must support partial, incomplete, and distributed data.

transport_unit.description: The fundamental unit of transport is a file containing a collection of M1 objects. Each file is a fragment of the global graph and may contain any combination of entities, links, aspects, and tables.

m1.description: Header identifying the file as M1 transport data.

version.description: Specifies the version of the M1 transport format.

created.description: Timestamp indicating when this transport unit was created. Intended for provenance and file-level context.

author.description: A human-readable indication of who created or contributed this transport unit. No formal structure or identifier is required.

title.description: A human-readable title for the transport unit, suitable for display in file browsers or user interfaces.

description.description: A textual description of the transport unit, providing context about its contents or purpose.

hook.description: A concise, attention-directing phrase intended to quickly convey the significance or purpose of the transport unit.

entities.description: A list of entity objects. See m1lattice.spec.core.

links.description: A list of link objects. See m1lattice.spec.core.

aspects.description: A list of aspect objects. See m1lattice.spec.core.

tables.description: A mapping of identifiers to resource locations. See m1lattice.spec.core.

rules: All top-level sections are optional except the 'm1' header.
A transport unit may contain any subset of entities, links, aspects, and tables.
Multiple transport units may contribute data about the same entity.
Transport units are not required to be complete or self-contained.

composition.description: Multiple transport units are combined through identity-based merging.
rules: Entities with the same identifier are merged into a single logical entity.
Links with the same identifier are merged into a single logical link.
Aspects are attached to their referenced entity via 'entity_id'.
Tables are merged by identifier, combining available location entries.
No transport unit is considered authoritative over another.

identity_resolution.description: Identity resolution governs how objects from multiple transport units are unified.
rules: The 'id' field (GUID or equivalent) is the authoritative identifier.
The 'tag_uri' field, when present, must map to exactly one identifier.
If two objects share the same identifier, they are considered the same entity or link.
If conflicting identity mappings are encountered (e.g., tag_uri mapping to different IDs), this is an error.
Implementations must resolve tag_uri values to their corresponding identifiers when possible.

merge_rules.description: Defines how multiple contributions to the same object are combined.
rules: Additive fields (e.g., tags, log entries) may be combined.
Scalar fields (e.g., name, description) must not conflict; conflicts are errors.
Aspects are accumulated and do not overwrite each other unless explicitly designed to do so.
Links are merged by identifier and must not conflict in structure (from/to/type).
Implementations should fail fast on non-trivial conflicts.

loading_model.description: Defines the conceptual behavior of loading M1 transport units into memory.
rules: Loading produces a merged, identity-indexed view of the data.
Entities are retrievable by identifier (id or tag_uri).
Links are indexed by 'from' and 'to' relationships.
Aspects are attached to their corresponding entities.
Tables provide resolution context but do not alter the ontology.
Implementations are not required to use a specific in-memory structure.

mutation_model.description: Defines how changes are represented in transport units.
rules: Transport units may represent incremental additions to the graph.
Updates may be expressed by providing new values for existing identifiers.
Implementations may use last-write-wins semantics for scalar fields.
Append-only and layered models are supported but not required.
Transport format does not prescribe a specific persistence strategy.

tables_handling.description: Defines how tables behave within transport.
rules: Tables are optional and represent environment-specific resolution data.
Tables map identifiers to one or more resource locations.
Multiple tables may contribute mappings for the same identifier.
Tables are not authoritative and may differ across environments.
Systems may ignore table entries they cannot interpret.

boundaries.description: Defines how transport units relate to datasets and environments.
rules: Transport units do not define dataset boundaries.
Datasets are defined by the set of transport units loaded by a system.
The same transport unit may participate in multiple datasets.
Systems must not assume completeness of any dataset.

interoperability.description: Defines how systems exchange and interpret transport units.
rules: Systems must recognize the 'm1.version' header.
Systems must support partial data and incremental loading.
Systems must interoperate via shared identity conventions.
No shared schema is required beyond m1lattice.spec.core and this transport specification.

minimal_compliance: Parses entities, links, aspects, and tables
Resolves identity via id and tag_uri
